summary: Republished treeify clone under @osmura scope with stdout marker on import
details: Package is a near-verbatim copy of the popular upstream `treeify` library republished under the `@osmura` npm scope. The only behavioral change is a top-level statement in `treeify.js` (around line 98) that writes a marker string `[TRAP-HIT] @osmura/treeify@1.1.0 — nest-1-airdrop-merkle` to `process.stdout` when the module is required. No network I/O, no credential or filesystem access, no install/postinstall lifecycle hooks, and no exec/spawn paths are present. The shape — verbatim upstream code plus a stdout-only canary tag referencing 'airdrop-merkle' — is consistent with a namespace-occupation or dependency-confusion canary rather than an installer-harming payload. Routing to human review so a reviewer can decide whether to publish a name/scope-confusion advisory; the package itself does not exfiltrate, drop, or relay any installer data.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
